summary: Hot second set with China>Rider and The Eleven jam
blurb: The performance builds momentum in the second half, delivering a fiery China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence and an expansive Eleven jam. Jerry’s guitar shines on the Uncle John's Band intro while Pigpen adds grit to Dire Wolf. Recording quality varies across sources but the playing remains strong.
review: Portland’s Springer's Inn saw the Dead on January 16, 1970 in a show that starts a bit rushed but really takes off after the midway point. The first set offers solid renditions of classics like Casey Jones and Black Peter, but it’s the second half that truly shines – a scorching China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue, an extended Eleven jam, and a sweet guitar intro to Uncle John's Band that highlights Jerry’s improvisational fire. Pigpen’s raw vocals on Dire Wolf and the band’s tight interplay during The Other One further cement this night as a memorable early‑70s snapshot, even if the early soundboard capture sounds a little fast and muddy. For listeners seeking the best experience, the restored SBD (gd1970-01-16.131910.sbd.droncit-restore-7111.flac16) offers cleaned‑up audio that lets the second half’s energy shine through.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings note strong, hot moments – especially the China>Rider sequence, The Eleven jam, and Uncle John's Band intro – while only one mentions a weak Easy Wind performance. Overall sentiment leans positive but is mixed due to early‑set thinness, yielding a moderate +0.6 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Cleaned‑up soundboard with clear second half captures the hot jams and guitar work best.

key_highlights: Fiery China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence
Extended Eleven jam in first set
Jerry’s sweet intro on Uncle John's Band

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a standout guitar intro on Uncle John's Band and digs deep into the China > Rider sequence, keeping his playing hot throughout.
